# libraries
```{r}
library(microViz)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(vegan)
library(data.table)
library(tidyverse)
```

# read phyloseq
```{r}
ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v3.rds')
#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_dups_samples_batch_check_all_physeq_wgs.rds')
#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_RESEQUENCING_wgs.rds')
#ps@sam_data$Age = as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Age)
#ps@sam_data$Weight <- as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Weight)
#ps@sam_data$Height <- as.numeric(ps@sam_data$Height)
#saveRDS(ps, '/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v3.rds')
#meta <- read_csv('metadata_eng_prepared.csv')
ps <- ps
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
level = 'Species'
taxas <- core_members(ps, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = F)
ps <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps)
ps <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE)
```

# plots
```{r}
# Define severity groups and taxonomic ranks
group_col <- "Batch"
#ranks <- c("Family", "Genus", "Species")
ranks <- c("Family")

# Generate and save plots for each taxonomic rank
for (rank in ranks) {
  ps_agg <- tax_glom(ps_obj, rank, NArm = TRUE)
  
  # Get top taxa
  top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps_agg), decreasing = TRUE)[1:30])
  ps_top <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps_agg)
  # Create plot for this rank
  p_list <- comp_barplot(
    ps_top,
    tax_level = rank,
    n_taxa = 30,
    group_by = group_col,
    merge_other = TRUE,
    sample_order = "aitchison",
    bar_outline_colour = NA,
    bar_width = 0.9
  )
  
  # Create a unified theme for all elements
  unified_theme <- theme(
    # Grid lines
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    # Titles and text
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    
    # Legend
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm")
  )
  
  # Apply unified theme to each plot
  p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
    p + 
      unified_theme
  })
  
  # Add grid to each plot in the list
  p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
    p + theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  })
  
  # Combine the group plots horizontally with shared legend
  combined_plot <- wrap_plots(p_list, ncol = length(p_list), guides = "collect") + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste("Top taxons at", rank, "level")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14))
  combined_plot <-  combined_plot & coord_flip()
  # Save the plot
  ggsave(paste0("batch_counts_top_30_", tolower(rank), "_RESEQ.png"), 
         combined_plot, 
         width = 28, height = 20, dpi = 300)
  
  # Print the plot
  print(combined_plot)
}
```
# microviz v3
```{r}
# Define severity groups and taxonomic ranks
group_col <- "Batch"
ranks <- c("Family")

# Generate and save plots for each taxonomic rank
for (rank in ranks) {
  ps_agg <- tax_glom(ps_obj, rank, NArm = TRUE)
  
  # Get top taxa
  top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps_agg), decreasing = TRUE)[1:30])
  ps_top <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps_agg)
  # Create plot for this rank
  p_list <- comp_barplot(
    ps_top,
    tax_level = rank,
    n_taxa = 30,
    group_by = group_col,
    merge_other = TRUE,
    sample_order = "aitchison",
    bar_outline_colour = NA,
    bar_width = 0.9
  )
  
  # Create a unified theme for all elements
  unified_theme <- theme(
    # Grid lines
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    # Titles and text
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size=24),
    
    # Legend
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm")
  )
  
  # Apply unified theme to each plot
  p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
    p + 
      unified_theme
  })
  
  # Add grid to each plot in the list
  p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
    p + theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  })
  
  # Combine the group plots horizontally with shared legend
  combined_plot <- wrap_plots(p_list, ncol = length(p_list), guides = "collect") + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste("Top taxons at", rank, "level")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 24))
  combined_plot <-  combined_plot & coord_flip()
  
  # THE FIX: Add this ONE LINE to make legend 1 column and size 24
  combined_plot <- combined_plot & guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1, 
                                                             title.theme = element_text(size = 34, face = "bold"),
                                                             label.theme = element_text(size = 34)))
  
  # Save the plot
  ggsave(paste0("/BATCH_counts_top_30_", tolower(rank), "_v4_BIGFIONT.png"), combined_plot,  width = 28, height = 20, dpi = 300)
  
  # Print the plot
  print(combined_plot)
}
```
# separate top taxa plots for each Timepoint
```{r}
# Define severity groups, taxonomic ranks, and timepoints
group_col <- "Group"
ranks <- c("Family")
timepoints <- c(-1, -2)

# Loop over timepoints
for (tp in timepoints) {
  
  # Filter phyloseq object for current timepoint
  ps_tp <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Timepoint == tp)
  n_samples <- nsamples(ps_tp)  # get sample count
  
  # Generate and save plots for each taxonomic rank
  for (rank in ranks) {
    ps_agg <- tax_glom(ps_tp, rank, NArm = TRUE)
    
    # Get top taxa
    top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps_agg), decreasing = TRUE)[1:30])
    ps_top <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps_agg)
    
    # Create plot for this rank
    p_list <- comp_barplot(
      ps_top,
      tax_level = rank,
      n_taxa = 30,
      group_by = group_col,
      merge_other = TRUE,
      sample_order = "aitchison",
      bar_outline_colour = NA,
      bar_width = 0.9
    )
    
    # Create a unified theme for all elements
    unified_theme <- theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm")
    )
    
    # Apply unified theme to each plot
    p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
      p + unified_theme
    })
    
    # Add grid to each plot in the list
    p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
      p + theme(
        panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
      )
    })
    
    # Combine the group plots horizontally with shared legend
    combined_plot <- wrap_plots(p_list, ncol = length(p_list), guides = "collect") + 
      plot_annotation(title = paste("Top taxa at", rank, "level - Timepoint", tp, "(n =", n_samples, ")")) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 4))
    combined_plot <-  combined_plot & coord_flip()
    
    # Make legend one column with larger text
    combined_plot <- combined_plot & guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1, 
                                                               title.theme = element_text(size = 44, face = "bold"),
                                                               label.theme = element_text(size = 44)))
    
    # Save the plot (optional – uncomment and adjust path as needed)
    ggsave(paste0("counts_top_30_", tolower(rank), "_tp", tp, "BIGFONT_.png"), 
            combined_plot, 
            width = 28, height = 20, dpi = 300)
    
    # Print the plot
    print(combined_plot)
  }
}
```

#timepoint shared legend color
```{r}
# Load libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(microViz)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
#ps <- readRDS('/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v3.rds')

bad_batch_samples <- c("AO-203-2", "AO-207-1", "B-022-2", "MB124-2")

if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps_obj <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}

group_col <- "Group"
ranks <- c("Family")
timepoints <- c(-1, -2)

# ---- Step 1: Store all plots and collect fill levels ----
all_plots <- list()
all_fill_levels <- c()

for (tp in timepoints) {
  ps_tp <- subset_samples(ps_obj, Timepoint == tp)
  n_samples <- nsamples(ps_tp)
  
  for (rank in ranks) {
    ps_agg <- tax_glom(ps_tp, rank, NArm = TRUE)
    top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps_agg), decreasing = TRUE)[1:30])
    ps_top <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps_agg)
    
    p_list <- comp_barplot(
      ps_top,
      tax_level = rank,
      n_taxa = 30,
      group_by = group_col,
      merge_other = TRUE,
      sample_order = "aitchison",
      bar_outline_colour = NA,
      bar_width = 0.9
    )
    
    unified_theme <- theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm")
    )
    p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) p + unified_theme)
    
    all_plots[[paste0("tp", tp)]] <- p_list
    
    # Extract fill levels from the first plot of this group
    fill_levels <- levels(p_list[[1]]$data[[rank]])
    all_fill_levels <- union(all_fill_levels, fill_levels)
  }
}

# ---- Step 2: Create maximally distinct colors ----
all_fill_levels <- sort(all_fill_levels)
n_col <- length(all_fill_levels)
cat("Number of unique fill levels (taxa + 'Other'):", n_col, "\n")

# Generate colors: full hue circle, max saturation, alternating brightness
hues <- seq(0, 360, length.out = n_col + 1)[1:n_col]  # evenly spaced hues
brightness <- rep(c(0.9, 0.6, 0.8, 0.5, 0.7), length.out = n_col)  # alternate light/dark
colors <- hsv(h = hues/360, s = 1, v = brightness)
names(colors) <- all_fill_levels

# Optional: preview colors
# scales::show_col(colors, ncol = 10)

cat("Number of colors generated:", length(colors), "\n")

# ---- Step 3: Re-apply color scale to every plot and save ----
for (tp in timepoints) {
  p_list <- all_plots[[paste0("tp", tp)]]
  n_samples <- nsamples(subset_samples(ps_obj, Timepoint == tp))
  rank <- ranks[1]
  
  # Apply manual fill scale; suppress warnings about replacing existing scale
  p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
    suppressWarnings(
      p + scale_fill_manual(values = colors, drop = FALSE, name = rank)
    )
  })
  
  p_list <- lapply(p_list, function(p) {
    p + theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.1),
              panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  })
  
  combined_plot <- wrap_plots(p_list, ncol = length(p_list), guides = "collect") + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste("Top taxa at", rank, "level - Timepoint", tp, "(n =", n_samples, ")")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 24))
  combined_plot <- combined_plot & coord_flip()
  
  combined_plot <- combined_plot & guides(fill = guide_legend(
    ncol = 1,
    title.theme = element_text(size = 44, face = "bold"),
    label.theme = element_text(size = 44)
  ))
  
  ggsave(paste0("counts_top_30_", tolower(rank), "_tp", tp, "_BIGFONT.png"), 
         combined_plot, width = 28, height = 20, dpi = 300)
  print(combined_plot)
}
```


# plots with ggplot2 (alt method if microViz shits its pants)
```{r}
for (rank in ranks) {
  # Aggregate at specified rank
  ps_agg <- tax_glom(ps_obj, rank, NArm = TRUE)
  
  # Get top taxa
  top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_sums(ps_agg), decreasing = TRUE)[1:30])
  ps_top <- prune_taxa(top_taxa, ps_agg)
  
  # Melt for plotting
  melted <- psmelt(ps_top)
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(melted, aes(y = Abundance, x = Sample, fill = get(rank))) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
    facet_wrap(~ get(group_col), scales = "free_x") +
    labs(fill = rank, title = paste("Top taxons at", rank, "level")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size=8))
  p 
  print(p)
  ggsave(paste0("counts_ggplot2_top_30_", tolower(rank), "_by_severity.png"), 
         p, width = 28, height = 14, dpi = 300)
}
```

# rarefaction curves with vegan
```{r}
ps_obj <- ps
ps_obj.prune = prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_obj) > 10, ps_obj)
readcount = data.table(as(sample_data(ps_obj.prune), "data.frame"),
                 TotalReads = sample_sums(ps_obj.prune), 
                 keep.rownames = TRUE)
#readcount <- readcount %>% 
  #filter(Timepoint == '-1')
#setnames(readcount, "rn", "SampleID")
ggplot(readcount, aes(TotalReads)) + geom_histogram() + ggtitle("Sequencing Depth")



head(readcount[order(readcount$TotalReads), c("SampleID", "TotalReads")])
otu.table <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(ps_obj.prune)))

r1 <- rarecurve(otu.table, step = 5000000, label = TRUE)
# Create rarefaction plot with colors
#r1 <- rarecurve(otu.table, step = 1000, label = FALSE)

# Get group information
sample_meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj.prune))
groups <- sample_meta$Group
group_colors <- c("A" = "red", "B" = "blue", "C" = "green")

# Create color vector for samples
sample_colors <- group_colors[as.character(groups)]

# Plot with colors
par(mar = c(5, 5, 4, 8), xpd = TRUE)  # Adjust margins for legend
rarecurve(otu.table, step = 5000000, col = sample_colors, lwd = 2,
          xlab = "Number of Reads", ylab = "Number of Species",
          main = "Rarefaction Curves by Group")

# Add legend
#legend("topright", inset = c(-0.2, 0), 
     #  legend = names(group_colors), 
     #  col = group_colors, 
     #  lwd = 2, 
     #  title = "Group")

# Check current read depths
sample_sums(ps_obj)

# Rarefy to minimum depth
#physeq_rarefied <- rarefy_even_depth(ps_obj, 
                                    # rngseed = 42, 
                                   #  replace = FALSE)

# Or rarefy to specific depth (e.g., 10,000)
#physeq_rarefied <- rarefy_even_depth(ps_obj, 
                                    # sample.size = 500000,
                                    # rngseed = 42)
```

#rarefaction but fancier
```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)

ps_obj <- ps
ps_obj.prune = prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_obj) > 1, ps_obj)
#ps_obj.filtered = subset_samples(ps_obj.prune, Timepoint == '-1')

# Get OTU table for filtered data
otu.table <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(ps_obj.prune)))

# Check if otu.table contains integers
print(str(otu.table[1:5, 1:5]))

# Run rarefaction with a smaller step size
r1 <- rarecurve(otu.table, step = 1000000, label = FALSE)

# Check the structure of r1
print(class(r1))
print(length(r1))
print(names(r1)[1:5])

# Check the structure of the first element
print(class(r1[[1]]))
print(dim(r1[[1]]))
print(head(r1[[1]]))

# Check if it's a vector or matrix
if(is.vector(r1[[1]])) {
  print("It's a vector")
  print(length(r1[[1]]))
  print(names(r1[[1]]))
} else if(is.matrix(r1[[1]])) {
  print("It's a matrix")
  print(dim(r1[[1]]))
  print(colnames(r1[[1]]))
}
```

# rareify pt3
```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)  # For better label placement

ps_obj <- ps
ps_obj.filtered = prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_obj) > 10, ps_obj)
#ps_obj.filtered = subset_samples(ps_obj.prune, Timepoint == '-1')

# Get OTU table and sample names
otu.table <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(ps_obj.filtered)))
sample_names <- rownames(otu.table)

# Run rarefaction
r1 <- rarecurve(otu.table, step = 5000000, label = FALSE)

# Extract data with sample names
rare_data <- data.frame()

for(i in seq_along(r1)) {
  if(length(r1[[i]]) > 0) {
    sample_name <- sample_names[i]
    curve_data <- r1[[i]]
    
    temp_df <- data.frame(
      Sample = sample_name,
      Reads = as.numeric(gsub("N", "", names(curve_data))),
      Species = as.numeric(curve_data)
    )
    
    rare_data <- rbind(rare_data, temp_df)
  }
}

# Get metadata
sample_meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_obj.filtered))
sample_meta$Sample <- rownames(sample_meta)
rare_data <- left_join(rare_data, sample_meta[, c("Sample", "Group")], by = "Sample")

# Define colors
group_colors <- c("A" = "#E41A1C", "B" = "#377EB8", "C" = "#4DAF4A")

# OPTION 1: Label ALL samples (might be crowded with 204 samples)
# Get the endpoint of each curve for labeling
end_points <- rare_data %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  filter(Reads == max(Reads)) %>%
  ungroup()

p1 <- ggplot(rare_data, aes(x = Reads, y = Species, group = Sample, color = Group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(data = end_points, 
                  aes(label = Sample),
                  size = 2.5,
                  max.overlaps = 200,  # Increase if you want more labels visible
                  segment.size = 0.2,
                  box.padding = 0.5,
                  show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors, na.value = "gray50") +
  labs(
    title = "Rarefaction Curves with Sample Labels",
    x = "Number of Reads",
    y = "Number of OTUs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)

# OPTION 2: Label only a subset of samples (e.g., one per group, or extreme values)
# Label the sample with highest species richness in each group
top_samples <- rare_data %>%
  group_by(Sample, Group) %>%
  summarise(Max_Species = max(Species), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  slice_max(Max_Species, n = 5) %>%  # Top 2 samples from each group
  ungroup()

# Filter endpoint data for these samples
end_points_subset <- end_points %>%
  filter(Sample %in% top_samples$Sample)

p2 <- ggplot(rare_data, aes(x = Reads, y = Species, group = Sample, color = Group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_label_repel(data = end_points_subset,
                   aes(label = Sample),
                   size = 3,
                   fill = "white",
                   alpha = 0.8,
                   box.padding = 0.5,
                   show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors, na.value = "gray50") +
  labs(
    title = "Rarefaction Curves (Labeled: Top Samples per Group)",
    x = "Number of Reads",
    y = "Number of OTUs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2)

# OPTION 3: Interactive plot with plotly (hover to see sample names)
# install.packages("plotly")
library(plotly)

p3 <- ggplot(rare_data, aes(x = Reads, y = Species, group = Sample, color = Group,
                            text = paste("Sample:", Sample, 
                                         "<br>Group:", Group,
                                         "<br>Reads:", Reads,
                                         "<br>OTUs:", round(Species, 0)))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors, na.value = "gray50") +
  labs(
    title = "Rarefaction Curves (Hover for Sample Details)",
    x = "Number of Reads",
    y = "Number of OTUs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Convert to interactive plot
ggplotly(p3, tooltip = "text")

# OPTION 4: Facet by group with a few labeled samples
# Get the sample with median richness in each group for labeling
median_samples <- rare_data %>%
  group_by(Sample, Group) %>%
  summarise(Max_Species = max(Species), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  mutate(Rank = rank(-Max_Species)) %>%
  filter(Rank == 3) %>%  # Label the 3rd highest in each group
  ungroup()

end_points_median <- end_points %>%
  filter(Sample %in% median_samples$Sample)

p4 <- ggplot(rare_data, aes(x = Reads, y = Species, group = Sample, color = Group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_label(data = end_points_median,
             aes(label = Sample),
             size = 3,
             hjust = 0,
             vjust = 0.5,
             label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines"),
             show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ Group, ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors, na.value = "gray50") +
  labs(
    title = "Rarefaction Curves by Group",
    x = "Number of Reads",
    y = "Number of OTUs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

print(p4)
```


# readcount
```{r}
# Create data frame with sample names, total reads, and group
readcount <- data.frame(
  SampleID = sample_names(ps_obj),
  TotalReads = sample_sums(ps_obj),
  Group = sample_data(ps_obj)$Group,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Order samples by Group only (preserving original order within groups)
readcount <- readcount[order(readcount$Group), ]
readcount$SampleID <- factor(readcount$SampleID, levels = readcount$SampleID)

# Create a numeric x position (1, 2, 3, ...) for manual labeling
readcount$x_num <- as.numeric(readcount$SampleID)

# Set a y coordinate for the labels (slightly below the bars)
label_y <- -0.05 * max(readcount$TotalReads)   # adjust the multiplier if needed

# Plot
p1 <- ggplot(readcount, aes(x = x_num, y = TotalReads, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 10e6, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  # Custom x‑axis labels: red if TotalReads < 10e6, otherwise black
  geom_text(aes(x = x_num, y = label_y, 
                label = SampleID, 
                color = ifelse(TotalReads < 10e6, "red", "black")),
            size = 3, angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5) +
  scale_color_identity() +                     # use the literal color names
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = readcount$x_num,                  # keep bars aligned
    labels = NULL,                             # hide default x‑axis numbers
    expand = c(0.02, 0)                        # optional extra space on sides
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +              # allow labels outside plot area
  labs(x = "Sample ID", y = "Total Reads", title = "Sequencing Depth per Sample") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),             # hide original x‑axis text
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),            # hide ticks
    plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 30, 5),         # increase bottom margin for labels
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("A" = "steelblue", "B" = "orange", "C" = "darkgreen"))
p1 <- p1 + annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 10e6, label = "10M cutoff", 
                    hjust = 1.1, vjust = -10.5, color = "red", size = 4)
# Save
ggsave(
  filename = "/WGS_readcount_v4.png",
  plot = p1,
  width = 28,
  height = 14,
  units = "in"
)
```

#readcount v3
```{r}
size=12
p1 <- ggplot(readcount, aes(x = SampleID, y = TotalReads, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",width=0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("Sequencing Depth by Sample") +
  xlab("Sample ID") +
  ylab("Number of Reads") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        legend.text = element_text(size = size+25),
    legend.title = element_text(size = size + 25))
p1

p2 <- ggplot(readcount, aes(x = SampleID, y = TotalReads, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("Sequencing Depth by Sample (log scale)") +
  xlab("Sample ID") +
  ylab("Number of Reads (log10)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma_format())
p2
ggsave(
  filename = paste0("/readcount_v3.png"),
  plot = p1,
  width = 28,
  height = 14,
  units = "in"
)
```

# metadata stacked barplot
```{r}
# Get categorical variables (already determined)
# Remove 'timepoint' and 'Group' (assuming 'Group' is your group variable name)
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))

# Check the structure of Timepoint column
cat("Class of Timepoint column:", class(meta$Timepoint), "\n")
cat("Unique values in Timepoint:", unique(meta$Timepoint), "\n")

meta <- meta %>% 
  filter(Timepoint == '-1')

cat("\nNumber of samples in Timepoint == '-1':", nrow(meta), "\n")

# Using the entire meta dataframe (no timepoint filtering)
categorical_vars <- names(meta)[sapply(meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]

# Remove 'timepoint' from the list if it exists
# Remove 'Group' from categorical_vars since that's our grouping variable
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, c('Age',"Group", 'Height', 'No_Patient','Weight','Timepoint','SampleID'))

# Check what we have
cat("Group variable: Group\n")
cat("Number of groups:", length(unique(meta$Group)), "\n")
cat("Categorical variables to plot:", length(categorical_vars), "\n")
print(categorical_vars)
cat("\nTotal observations in dataset:", nrow(meta), "\n")

# Select only the columns we need
df_f <- meta %>%
  select(Group, all_of(categorical_vars))

# Create long format while preserving Group
df_long <- df_f %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Group,
               names_to = "Column_Name",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  # Remove rows with NA values in Value
  filter(!is.na(Value))

# Calculate percentages WITHIN each Value (category level)
# This means for each categorical variable value, what's the distribution of Groups?
df_summary <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Column_Name, Value, Group) %>%
  tally() %>%
  group_by(Column_Name, Value) %>%  # Group by Column_Name and Value for percentages
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    percent = n/total * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Plotting parameters - adjust as needed
size <- 15  # Base text size

# Create the plot: 100% stacked bars with Group as fill, x = Value
p <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = Value, y = percent, fill = Group)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n)),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = size/3, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ Column_Name, scales = "free", ncol = 7) +
  labs(title = "Percentage Distribution by Group for Each Categorical Variable",
       subtitle = paste("Total N =", nrow(meta)),
       x = "Category Values",
       y = "Percentage",
       fill = "Group") +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = size+5, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = size+5, color = "black", angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.title = element_text(size = size + 5, color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = size+35),
    legend.title = element_text(size = size + 35),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.2),
    strip.text = element_text(size = size, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray80", color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = size + 5, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = size)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("A" = "#2c7bb6", "B" = "#d7191c", "C" = "#1a9641")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1)))

# Display the plot
print(p)

# Save to PDF
if (length(categorical_vars) > 0) {
  pdf_width <- min(45, 8 + 7 * ceiling(length(categorical_vars)/4))
  pdf_height <- min(70, 5 * ceiling(length(categorical_vars)/4))
  
  pdf("metadata_percentage_by_group_stacked_v3.pdf", width = pdf_width, height = pdf_height)
  print(p)
  dev.off()
  
  cat("\nPDF file saved: metadata_percentage_by_group_stacked.pdf\n")
} else {
  cat("\nNo categorical variables to plot!\n")
}
```
